description:
## Goal

Lay the canonical-first foundation purely as an *observation* layer.
Populate per-fn canonical slots + per-slot inflow histograms during
typing, alongside the existing per-callsite spec discipline. Nothing
reads the new data yet. All existing tests stay green. No golden
changes.

This isolates the new data model + projection move from the demolition
that follows.

## What lands

### Move shared primitive into the lattice
- src/types.rs: add
  ```
  pub enum ArgRepr { Tagged, RawInt, RawF64, Condition }
  impl ArgRepr {
      pub fn from_descr(d: &Descr) -> ArgRepr { ... }
  }
  ```
  Moved from src/ir_codegen.rs:778. `for_block_param` and `cl_type`
  stay in codegen (codegen-shape decisions, not lattice projection).
- src/ir_codegen.rs: re-export / import from `crate::types::ArgRepr`.
  Net behavioral diff: zero.

### Canonical data model
- src/ir_typer.rs:
  ```
  pub struct Canonical {
      pub fid: FnId,
      pub slots: Vec<CanonicalSlot>,
  }
  pub struct CanonicalSlot {
      pub descr: Descr,
      pub inflows: HashMap<ArgRepr, usize>,
  }
  ```
- `ModuleTypes` gains `pub canonical: HashMap<FnId, Canonical>`.

### Population
- In `walk_spec_for_discovery::push_key` (src/ir_typer.rs:892), AFTER
  the existing per-callsite minting and pending-set bookkeeping:
  - Look up / lazily create `canonical[F]`. First observation sizes
    `slots` to `K.len()` with `descr = Descr::never()` (or first-
    observation lazy init if `never()` unavailable).
  - For each i: `slots[i].descr = slots[i].descr.union(&K[i])`.
  - For each i: `*slots[i].inflows.entry(ArgRepr::from_descr(&K[i]))
    .or_default() += 1`.

### Debug-assert invariant
At end of `type_module`, for each canonical / each slot:

  `inflows.keys()` ⊆ reachable_reprs(slot.descr)

Cheap equivalent form:
  - If `ArgRepr::from_descr(&slot.descr) != Tagged`,
    then `inflows.keys() == {ArgRepr::from_descr(&slot.descr)}`.
  - Tagged-LUB slots accept any inflow repr.

`debug_assert!` only — no release cost.

## What does NOT change in this ticket

- `specs: HashMap<(FnId, Vec<Descr>), FnTypes>` stays.
- `effective_returns` stays composite-keyed.
- `naturally_opaque`, `any_key_specs`, `ensure_any_keys` stay.
- `SpecRegistry` two-tier resolve stays.
- All codegen ABI bookkeeping (`tagged_return_specs`, etc.) stays.
- `reachable_specs` stays.
- All goldens stay byte-identical.

## Acceptance

- `cargo build` clean; no clippy warnings.
- All 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests pass with NO golden changes.
- `ModuleTypes::canonical` populated for every FnId reached by the
  fixpoint.
- Debug-assert invariant holds across the full test suite.
- `ArgRepr` lives in `types.rs`; codegen imports it from there.

## Walkthrough risk notes

- `Descr::never()` bottom element: confirm it exists at `types.rs`. If
  not, use `Option<Descr>` for the slot until first observation, then
  collapse to `Some(_)` permanently.
- Slot count grows monotonically? No — fixed at arity by first
  push_key. Subsequent push_keys must match arity; `debug_assert_eq!`
  the lengths.

## See also

- fz-7ot — epic parent (canonical-first).
- fz-7ot.2 — demolition; blocks on this.
- src/ir_typer.rs:892 — push_key insertion point.
- src/types.rs:590 — Descr::union.
title: fz-7ot.1: canonical model + LUB observation (additive)
